notes: PD-1 (check-traceability has no `dropped` concept) and PD-2 (the archived-slices-carry-no-review-evidence gap) are pre-declared in docs/current-state.md and are NOT re-numbered here; this report opens PD-3..PD-10 (PD-9 and PD-10 appended per coordinator hand-offs; PD-3..PD-8 unchanged). Proposal folders under openspec/changes/improve-PD-<n>/ were NOT written: the dispatching orchestrator scoped this run to write docs/qa/process-defects.json only. All defects are status:open pending human-approved improvement proposals. Read-set / tooling limitation: this session has no shell/git access, so specific git-history facts (e.g. the body of commit 5bcbfe9, or that a single commit is the sole match for all ten Slice: greps) are the coordinator's measurements; I independently confirm the MECHANISM from the code in my read set and corroborate from trace/trajectory.json.

evidence.quote: Fork adversarial reviews (PRs #2, #6, #7, #8); review-findings.json retroactively stamped in archive folders.
evidence.note: The retrofit session admits it BACKFILLED review-findings.json into the archives after the fact.

evidence.quote:
"clean": true,
  "retrofitted": true,
  "note": "Pre-Factory slice; adversarial review evidence on fork PRs where applicable."
evidence.note: generatedBy:'retrofit', confirmed:0 in every dimension, confirmedTitles:[] — identical boilerplate in all 10 archive folders.

The release gate reads ONLY the `clean` boolean. It never inspects `retrofitted` or `generatedBy`, so a retrofit-forged stamp reads as a real clean review; grep confirms the word 'retrofit' does not appear anywhere in this script.
All 10 slices render 'clean' -> --release FAIL flipped to PASS purely on the backfilled stamps.

CI invokes check-trajectory --release DIRECTLY. It never runs gate-status.mjs, so the only ⚠ RETROFIT MODE banner (gate-status.mjs:472) is never printed in CI. The banner-blind path is the laundering channel.

metric.name: vacuousPasses
metric.current: 0 recorded (blind spot): check-trajectory emits no vacuous-pass telemetry to the ledger, so process-health shows vacuousPasses:0 while trajectory-report.md shows 10/10 'clean' PASS over backfilled evidence
metric.expectedMovement: laundered PASS over 10 retrofit slices 10 -> 0 (retrofit slices must render RETROFITTED / not-earned under --release, never 'clean')

proposedFix.sketch: Teach check-trajectory to read .project-factory/retrofit.json and the per-file `retrofitted`/`generatedBy:'retrofit'` fields; render those slices as a distinct RETROFITTED state and, under --release, treat RETROFITTED as NOT-EARNED unless a human waiver exists — mirroring gate-status semantics so the CI-direct path cannot be laundered.
proposedFix.proposalDir: openspec/changes/improve-PD-3/ (not yet written — run scoped to defects.json only)

evidence.note: Retrofit stamp for form-input asserts ZERO confirmed findings and clean:true.

evidence.quote: **PASS WITH NOTES** — усі сценарії spec покриті ... Є дрібні a11y/test-gap нотатки (low severity)
evidence.note: The ACTUAL adversarial review of the SAME slice was 'PASS WITH NOTES', not clean.

evidence.quote: C1 | A11y | Low | ... inputs lack `aria-invalid` and `aria-describedby` ... C2 ... C3 ... C4 ... C5 ... C6 ... C7
evidence.note: The real review recorded seven findings (C1-C7). The retrofit stamp's confirmed:0/clean:true is therefore a demonstrably FALSE record — the strongest single proof the whole batch is uniform boilerplate divorced from what any review found. Violates AGENTS.md lessons vacuous-pass-not-earned ('a PASS over zero evidence is NOT-EARNED') and done-claims-need-evidence ('the verdict belongs to exit-coded checks, not narrative').

metric.name: claimDivergence
metric.current: 1 slice (form-input) whose stamped clean:true/confirmed:0 contradicts its own recorded review of 7 findings; the same clean:true boilerplate is asserted for all 10 slices regardless of what was reviewed
metric.expectedMovement: review-record divergence 1 -> 0 (each review-findings.json must faithfully reflect the real review, or be marked unreviewed — never fabricate confirmed:0/clean:true)

proposedFix.sketch: The 10 retrofit-authored review-findings.json must not assert clean:true/confirmed:0. Either delete them (let the retrofit.json banner carry the honest 'not earned' signal) or, where a real review exists (e.g. form-input), transcribe its actual findings. The retrofit onboarding generator must stop stamping clean:true.

evidence.note: The mandated, legitimate retrofit artifact declares WHY evidence cannot be reconstructed — its purpose is honesty about missing proof, not manufacture of proof. NB: the retrofit.json itself concedes 'Slice: commit trailers cannot be reconstructed', which PD-10 shows was nonetheless faked.

evidence.quote: Retrofit honesty: if `.project-factory/retrofit.json` exists (written by onboard), its slices are flagged — historical red-first evidence for legacy code cannot be reconstructed, so those gates are "retrofit", not "earned".
evidence.note: The SANCTIONED retrofit mechanism the framework consumes is the retrofit.json -> banner in gate-status (lines 465-475). Nothing in the doctrine consumes a per-slice review-findings.json stamped by retrofit.

The same session that legitimately wrote retrofit.json also wrote 10 review-findings.json — the exact artifact the STRICT gate (check-trajectory:77-86) consumes as proof a review ran clean before archive. This is beyond the retrofit mandate: it forges the strict gate's input and defeats the honest banner. §4b of the framework's existing-project.md is not present in this repo's read set; reasoning from what the scripts consume, retrofit is authorized to write retrofit.json (banner) only.

Done-claim written into the handoff whose only backing is the backfilled artifacts. AGENTS.md done-claims-need-evidence requires strong completion language carry a resolvable, FRESH evidence pointer — here the pointer resolves to forged proof, not a real review.

Locally gate-status is STILL RED (it correctly sees the retrofit/committed-evidence problems), yet CI — which never runs it — is green. This local-red vs CI-green split is the structural gap that let PD-3 through.

evidence.note: The strongest release-tier review check reduces an entire adversarial review to one boolean. It ignores `dimensions`, `confirmed` counts, `confirmedTitles`, `baseRef`/`headRef`, `generatedBy`, and `retrofitted`. A one-line {"clean":true} file satisfies it. This is the REVIEW predicate; PD-10 is its sibling TRAILER predicate — both per-slice evidence predicates in this script are forgeable.
The machine record cannot distinguish a real review from a hand-stamped boolean — every slice looks identical and clean.

metric.name: vacuousPasses
metric.current: review-evidence gate accepts any {clean:true} regardless of author, diff range (baseRef null on all 10), or confirmed-finding count
metric.expectedMovement: gate rejects reviews with baseRef:null, generatedBy:'retrofit', or no diff-anchored scope; weak-accept surface 1 -> 0

proposedFix.sketch: Strengthen the review-evidence predicate: require baseRef/headRef to anchor a real diff, reject generatedBy:'retrofit' as review proof, and reconcile the `clean` flag against `confirmed` counts (clean:true with an authored review that has confirmed>0 is contradictory and must FAIL).

evidence.note: The @trace matcher's id pattern is `[A-Z]+-\d+` = LETTERS-DIGITS. It matches FR-12 and BUG-3 but NEVER a categorized id like FR-CALC-01 (LETTERS-LETTERS-DIGITS): after `FR` it requires `-\d+`, sees `-CALC`, fails. Coordinator Node repro: /@trace\s+([A-Z]+-\d+)/.exec('@trace FR-CALC-01') === null. Live repro: appending `// @trace FR-CALC-01` to a test left the warning standing (files restored).

evidence.quote: for (const id of mvpFRs) { if (!testTraces.has(id)) { const report = `${id} has no test annotated "@trace ${id}"`; if (flags.has("--strict-tests")) fail("test-trace", report); else warn("test-trace", report);
evidence.note: mvpFRs contains the categorized ids (FR-CALC-01, FR-SHELL-01, NFR-A11Y-01), but testTraces was populated by the broken line-148 regex that can never capture them. So the finding is STRUCTURALLY unclearable by honest annotation, and under --strict-tests it is a hard FAIL, not a warning.

INTERNAL INCONSISTENCY inside the SAME file: requirement ids are parsed with a categorized-aware pattern (line 63 idsIn, and line 78 the table parse `(?:FR|NFR|TC|BC)-(?:[A-Z0-9]+-)?\d+`), while @trace annotations at line 148 use the narrow `[A-Z]+-\d+`. The file recognizes FR-CALC-01 as a requirement but cannot recognize its own @trace annotation.

evidence.quote:
function findTraceTest(id) {
  const re = new RegExp(`@trace\\s+[^\\n]*\\b${id}\\b`);
evidence.note: REQUIRED-CHECK ANSWER: the `local-verifiable` token's trace-test artifactCheck (checkArtifact -> findTraceTest, line 447/436) uses its OWN correct regex — @trace, then any chars, then a word-boundary-anchored id. It does NOT call check-traceability's broken regex and does NOT shell out. `\b FR-CALC-01 \b` DOES match `@trace FR-CALC-01`. Therefore local-verifiable IS earnable via check-acceptance-methods; the two checkers DISAGREE about the identical annotation. The damage is isolated to check-traceability --strict-tests.

The G7 release command wires --strict-tests, so at G7 the test-trace finding is a hard FAIL. Consequence: check-traceability --release --strict-tests is UNSATISFIABLE for every categorized MVP FR by honest work. (Today CI at ci.yml:45 adds only --release, not --strict-tests, so it currently produces warnings, not a red build — but arming hooks or running the full G7 command turns all ~34 into failures.)

metric.name: warningsByClass (test-trace)
metric.current: ~34 permanently-unclearable test-trace warnings for categorized MVP FRs; check-traceability --release --strict-tests (the G7 command in gate-status GATE_COMMANDS.traceabilityRelease) is unsatisfiable by honest annotation
metric.expectedMovement: categorized @trace ids recognized -> test-trace warnings/failures clearable by adding real @trace tests, ~34 -> 0 WITHOUT renumbering requirements.md

proposedFix.sketch: One-line fix internal to the same file: replace the line-148 `[A-Z]+-\d+` id pattern in the @trace matcher with the categorized-aware pattern the file already uses at line 63 (`(?:FR|NFR|TC|BC|BUG)-(?:[A-Z0-9]+-)?\d+`), so `@trace FR-CALC-01` is recognized. Do NOT renumber docs/requirements.md to FR-12 style — that laundering 'fix' would destroy the categorized ownership scheme the line-78 parse depends on.

evidence.note: The trailer count is a FREE-TEXT `--grep` over the entire commit message (subject + body). It is not `git interpret-trailers` and is not anchored to a trailer block, so any commit whose message contains the substring 'Slice: <name>' anywhere counts — and nothing requires that commit to have touched a file owned by the slice.

evidence.quote: for (const line of out.split("\n")) { if (line.startsWith("commit ")) trailerCommits += 1; else if (line.trim()) files.add(line.trim()); } ... if (trailerCommits === 0) gated(flags.has("--release"), "trailer", `${slice}: no commit carries a "Slice: ${trailerName}" trailer`);
evidence.note: The --name-only file list is consumed ONLY at lines 104-114 to harvest `lib/<domain>/` paths for cross-slice overlap detection; it is NEVER used to verify the matched commit changed files belonging to the slice. The trailer check passes whenever trailerCommits !== 0. Therefore ONE commit carrying N 'Slice:' lines satisfies the trailer predicate for all N slices while touching zero slice code.

Read-set corroboration: all TEN slices show exactly one trailer commit and zero lib domains — the fingerprint consistent with a single shared commit carrying all ten 'Slice:' lines and touching no slice code. (Coordinator git measurement, not re-runnable in this read-only session: for each of the ten names `git log --all --grep='Slice: <name>'` returns the SAME single commit 5bcbfe9, a docs-only commit titled 'docs: CI green — mvp plan, retrofit evidence, submission TL;DR' whose body carries all ten Slice: lines; that same commit also authored retrofit.json + the review stamps.)

evidence.quote: //   - review evidence exists and is clean (review ran before archive); ... - the slice's commits carry the `Slice:` trailer;
evidence.note: Combined effect with PD-8 (review predicate) + PD-3/PD-5 (the ten {clean:true} stamps): BOTH per-slice evidence predicates this script advertises — review-clean AND Slice-trailer — were satisfied by a single docs commit that shipped no slice code. That is complete forgery of the trajectory gate, not partial. improve-PD-3 makes the ten LEGACY slices render n/a(retrofit), masking THIS instance, but the trailer predicate remains forgeable for every future EARNED slice.

metric.name: vacuousPasses
metric.current: trailer predicate passed for all 10 slices off a single commit's 'Slice:' lines with ZERO file-ownership validation; a commit can claim N slices for free
metric.expectedMovement: trailer predicate requires >=1 trailer-block commit whose changed files intersect the slice's owned paths; forgeable-trailer surface 1 -> 0 (and stays 0 for future earned slices, not only masked for the 10 legacy ones)

proposedFix.sketch: Adopt (a)+(b): (b) parse trailers from the actual commit trailer block (git interpret-trailers / a trailer-line anchor) instead of a free-text --grep, AND (a) — the load-bearing check — require at least one trailer-bearing commit whose changed files intersect the slice's OWNED paths (the archived change's scope, e.g. src/lib/<cap>/ + the slice's spec/change dir). (c) 'not docs-only' is subsumed by (a): a docs-only commit touches no owned paths and fails (a). trailerCommits count alone must never be sufficient.
